description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) analysis revealing the orientation dependence of deformation twinning in Fe-35Mn-6Al-0.1C low-carbon low-density steel during tensile deformation at room temperature (RT) and liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT). The upper row (a1-d1) displays inverse pole figures along the tensile axis direction, where red, green, and blue dots represent Type I, II, and III grain orientations respectively, with regions satisfying m_tw < m_s (twinning Schmid factor less than slip Schmid factor) demarcated by curves. The lower row (a2-d2) shows corresponding EBSD inverse pole orientation maps. The panels compare microstructures at RT with 38% strain (a1,a2), LNT with 28% strain (b1,b2), LNT with 38% strain (c1,c2), and LNT with 55% strain (d1,d2). The data demonstrate that deformation twins preferentially form in Type II and III grains, with the twinning fraction increasing with strain at LNT. Notably, Type II grains exhibit single-orientation twinning while Type III grains tend to form multiple-orientation twinning. The figure supports that deformation twinning in this steel is strongly orientation-dependent but does not strictly follow Schmid's law, as Type II grains display extensive twinning despite having lower twinning Schmid factors than slip Schmid factors.
